scope.summary:
Goal: Sample CPU%, resident memory (RSS), and GPU/driver identity as periodic typed `internal_diagnostics` RESOURCE COUNTERS (Master Spec v02.196 §5.8.2 'resource counters'). The §6.13.1 'pinned under heavy CPU' stall mode means CPU% is a first-class signal Palmistry/the panel care about. CPU + RSS are sampled periodically off the hot frame path; GPU adapter info is read ONCE at startup from the already-initialized wgpu render state (it is static identity, not a per-frame counter).

Exact files to create/edit:
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/Cargo.toml` — promote `sysinfo` from a [dev-dependencies] entry to a real [dependencies] entry. NOTE (version drift, VERIFY): handshake-native currently has `sysinfo = "0.33"` under [dev-dependencies] (used by the MT-045 perf suite). The current crates.io sysinfo is 0.39.5 (verified 2026-06-27). To avoid a DUPLICATE sysinfo family in the locked graph, pin the new runtime dep to the SAME line already locked (0.33) UNLESS the coder upgrades both the dev-dep and the runtime dep together to 0.39 in one step and re-runs `cargo tree -d` to confirm a single sysinfo core. Pick one and record it; do NOT leave two sysinfo majors in the graph. sysinfo gives cross-platform CPU% (per-process) + RSS (Windows GetProcessMemoryInfo, Linux /proc, macOS task_info) with no per-OS windows-sys feature add.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/diagnostics/resource_counters.rs` — a `ResourceSampler` that holds a `sysinfo::System` (refreshed for THIS process only — refresh_process(Pid::from_u32(std::process::id())) so we never read other processes' data, which would be both wasteful and a privacy concern) and a `sample() -> ResourceSample { cpu_milli: u64, rss_kb: u64 }` (cpu as integer milli-percent, rss in KB — typed integers). `record_sample()` calls `diagnostics::record_with(DiagEventCode::ResourceSample, ...)`.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/diagnostics/gpu_info.rs` — capture the wgpu adapter info ONCE. eframe with the wgpu renderer exposes the render state via `eframe::CreationContext::wgpu_render_state` (a `RenderState` with the `wgpu::Adapter`). In `HandshakeApp::new(cc)`, read `cc.wgpu_render_state` -> adapter `.get_info()` -> `AdapterInfo { name, vendor, device, device_type, driver, driver_info, backend }`. Map this to a TYPED record: vendor id (u32), device id (u32), device_type (enum -> u8), backend (enum -> u8). The driver NAME/INFO strings are vendor/driver identity (e.g. 'NVIDIA', 'D3D12') — field-standard hardware identity, NOT project data; they are allowed but keep them to a bounded set / store them once for the panel's hardware line, and DO NOT put strings into the typed ring DiagEvent (the ring is integers; emit the vendor/device/backend as integer codes via ResourceSample-adjacent fields, keep the human strings only in an in-process GpuInfo the panel reads). Document this split.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/app.rs` — (a) in `HandshakeApp::new`, capture GpuInfo once from cc.wgpu_render_state and store it; (b) drive periodic CPU/RSS sampling: sample at a bounded cadence (~1-2s, NOT every frame — CPU% needs an interval between refreshes to be meaningful and sysinfo refresh is not free) using a `last_sample_instant` gate inside `fn update`, OR a lightweight background timer; emit a ResourceSample event each interval. Sampling inside `fn update` behind a ~1s gate is the simplest correct approach (no extra thread) — but the sysinfo refresh must be cheap enough not to stutter the frame; if it is not, move the refresh to a `spawn_blocking` on the existing tokio runtime and only read the cached result on the frame. Choose + document.
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_resource_counters.rs`.

Reuse note: sysinfo is ALREADY a (dev-)dependency — reuse the same crate, just the right scope + version. wgpu is ALREADY the eframe renderer (eframe = { features = ["wgpu", ...] }) and `cc.wgpu_render_state` is the supported eframe seam to reach the adapter — no new wgpu dep, no second device. The tokio runtime + `fn update` are already there. src/backend untouched.

Risk if missed: no CPU/RSS counters means the §6.13.1 heavy-CPU stall mode is invisible in-process and the panel's resource line (§5.8.4) is empty; a wrong sampling cadence either stutters the frame (too frequent sysinfo refresh) or gives meaningless CPU% (no interval between refreshes).
acceptance_criteria: AC-006-1: a test constructs a ResourceSampler, samples CPU% + RSS for THIS process (sysinfo refresh_process on the current pid only), and asserts the sample returns plausible typed integers (rss_kb > 0; cpu_milli is a non-negative integer) — and that the sampler refreshes ONLY the current process (not the whole system process table).
AC-006-2: a ResourceSample DiagEvent reaches the ring/buffer (typed integers only — cpu_milli, rss_kb — no content) via `record_sample`.

AC-006-3: GPU adapter info is captured once from `cc.wgpu_render_state` — a kittest (or the wgpu-screenshot-gated harness) constructs the real HandshakeApp and asserts a non-empty GpuInfo (vendor/device/backend codes set) is stored; the human driver strings are kept in the in-process GpuInfo only, NOT pushed into the typed ring DiagEvent.

AC-006-4: sampling cadence is bounded (~1-2s, not per-frame) and does not stutter the frame — a kittest steps many frames and asserts ResourceSample is emitted at the bounded interval (not every frame) and the frame loop stays responsive (no SlowFrame storm from the sampler).
AC-006-5: NO duplicate sysinfo family in the locked graph — `cargo tree -d -p handshake-native` shows a single sysinfo core (the chosen pin is recorded; the dev-dep + runtime dep agree).
AC-006-6: `cargo build -p handshake-native` (lib + bins) clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full suite `-j 2` green; external target dir; no repo-local target/, no tracked artifact.
proof_targets: PT-006-A: `cargo test -p handshake-native resource_counters` GREEN — current-process CPU/RSS sample + ResourceSample ring event.
PT-006-B: GpuInfo captured from cc.wgpu_render_state (kittest / wgpu-gated) — vendor/device/backend codes non-empty.
PT-006-D: `cargo tree -d` single sysinfo core; `cargo build --lib --bins` clean + clippy clean + full suite -j 2 green.
implementation_notes: sysinfo: refresh ONLY the current process (sysinfo::System::new + refresh_process(Pid::from_u32(process::id())) or the ProcessesToUpdate::Some([pid]) form on 0.33+), never the whole system — cheaper and avoids reading other processes. CPU% needs an interval between two refreshes to be meaningful; the ~1-2s cadence provides it.

VERSION: handshake-native has sysinfo 0.33 as a dev-dep already. Either pin the new runtime dep to 0.33 (match the locked line, simplest, no dup) OR upgrade dev+runtime together to 0.39.5 and re-run cargo tree -d. Do NOT introduce a 0.33+0.39 duplicate. Record the decision in research_provenance.

GPU: eframe's wgpu RenderState is reachable via cc.wgpu_render_state in HandshakeApp::new; adapter.get_info() gives AdapterInfo. Map vendor/device/device_type/backend to integer codes for the typed ring; keep the human name/driver strings ONLY in an in-process GpuInfo for the panel's hardware line (hardware identity, not project data, but keep strings out of the integer ring).

Cadence: a ~1s last_sample_instant gate inside fn update is the simplest no-extra-thread approach; if sysinfo refresh is too heavy for the frame, move refresh to spawn_blocking on the existing tokio runtime and read the cached result on the frame (document which).
All ring fields are integers (cpu_milli, rss_kb, vendor_id, device_id, backend_code) — typed allowlist preserved.
Reuse the existing eframe wgpu renderer + tokio runtime + fn update; no new device, no new runtime, no new dependency family beyond promoting sysinfo's scope.
Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the counters must be REAL (sysinfo of the live process, real wgpu adapter), not stubbed constants.
ports_from_react: NONE (native resource sampling; no React analogue)
binds_backend_api: NONE. Resource counters are sampled in-process (sysinfo) + read from the local wgpu adapter. FR not involved.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: Resource counters are Tier 2 in-process diagnostics; not Tier 1 business events, not forwarded to FR.
This MT IS the Tier 2 resource-counter component (§5.8.2): live CPU/RSS sampling + GPU identity + typed ResourceSample events for the panel, wired into the live app. Built + consumed here.

Resource samples flow through the ring records; Palmistry's freeze/crash detection (MT-091/012) keys on the heartbeat + process handle, not the resource samples. (Palmistry MAY read the last-N records including ResourceSample for its survivor store via MT-093, but no Palmistry wiring is required of THIS MT.)

risks: RISK-006-1: a duplicate sysinfo family (0.33 dev-dep + 0.39 runtime dep) in the locked graph. Mitigate: pin one line for both; cargo tree -d single core (AC-006-5).
RISK-006-2: sampling every frame stutters the UI (sysinfo refresh is not free) or gives meaningless CPU% (no interval). Mitigate: bounded ~1-2s cadence; optional spawn_blocking refresh (AC-006-4).
RISK-006-3: reading the whole system process table leaks other-process data + wastes CPU. Mitigate: refresh only the current pid (AC-006-1).
RISK-006-4: GPU driver strings pushed into the typed ring break the integer-only allowlist. Mitigate: integer codes in the ring; human strings only in the in-process GpuInfo (AC-006-3).
RISK-006-5: a second wgpu device/adapter created instead of reusing eframe's. Mitigate: read cc.wgpu_render_state's existing adapter only.
minimum_controls: MC-006-2: bounded cadence, no frame stutter (AC-006-4).
MC-006-4: integer-only ring fields; strings confined to in-process GpuInfo (AC-006-3).

research_provenance.field_stack: sysinfo for cross-platform per-process CPU%/RSS (the canonical Rust system-metrics crate, already a handshake-native dev-dep); eframe wgpu RenderState adapter.get_info() for static GPU/driver identity (no second device).

crate_versions_verified_2026_06_27.pin_decision: pin the new runtime dep to the SAME line already locked (0.33) to avoid a duplicate family, OR upgrade dev+runtime to 0.39.5 together and re-verify cargo tree -d; coder records which.

research_provenance.motivating_incident: §6.13.1 names 'pinned under heavy CPU' as a stall mode; the 2026-06-26 freeze showed CPU->0 (the opposite extreme). CPU%/RSS counters make both extremes visible in-process and feed the panel's resource line.
research_provenance.validation_plan: current-process sample + ResourceSample ring event + GPU capture + bounded cadence + single sysinfo core; clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2.
